reasoning: The resolution date (July 1, 2025) is only 1.5 months away from the knowledge cutoff (mid-May 2025). While internal EPA communications indicate plans to "sunset" and eliminate the Energy Star program under the Trump administration, shutting down a 30-year-old federal program with over $100 billion in annual related commerce, international ties (Canada uses EPA's API for its own certifications), and embedded tax-credit infrastructure takes considerable time. Furthermore, Energy Star was codified into law via the Energy Policy Act of 2005, which could pose legal barriers or slow down the dismantling process. Even if the program is completely defunded, federal websites typically do not go completely offline (yielding a domain resolution or host connection failure) in a matter of weeks; at a minimum, the domain usually remains active to host an archive or an informational shutdown message. Therefore, it is highly probable that IsItDownRightNow will still read the domain as "UP" on July 1, 2025.
